results.title: Lantana (Special edition) [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Ray Lawrence, Mandy Walker, Jan Chapman, Film Finance Corporation Australia, MBP and Saddleback Productions
results.brief_description: Ray Lawrence (Bliss) returned to filmmaking after a sixteen-year absence to direct “Lantana”. Adapted from his own play, scriptwriter Andrew Bovell depicts a dozen characters, reflecting a prism of modern day Sydney. One woman’s death - perhaps murder - sparks a crisis in a series on interconnecting relationships. Leon is a policeman investigating the woman’s disappearance, who’s emotional stolidity is reflected in his violent dealings with clients; his aging wife Sonja seeks counselling for her desolate personal state; her therapist Valerie, is struggling to cope with the death of her teenage daughter and the deterioration of her marriage to John, an emotionally remote academic; Jane, a lonely and aimless woman, is estranged from her husband Pete and seeks sex with Leon. Miscommunication and secret trysts (real and imagined) leads characters to draw false conclusions. Several red herrings increase the intensity of the murder mystery, but the real concern of the film is the kaleidoscope of human failings and interactions, reflecting a complex interweaving of intent and desire. Paul Kelly’s score serves as an effective moody soundtrack to the film. Winner of seven AFI awards. Cinematography by Mandy Walker. Cast includes Anthony La Paglia, Geoffrey Rush, Barbara Hershey, Kerry Armstrong, Rachael Blake, Vince Colosimo, Peter Phelps, Leah Purcell, Glenn Robins.

results.title: The Entity
results.creator_credit: Sidney J. Furie, Harold Schneider, American Cinema Productions and Pelleport Investors
results.brief_description: This horror/haunted house/poltergeist movie is based on a true story of a woman who was harassed by inexplicable forces. Carla Moran (Barbara Hershey) is a caring mother who lives with her children, when she is repeatedly and brutally raped by an unseen entity, and besieged by other inconceivable events that petrify her. Carla is increasingly isolated (even ridiculed) for her experiences, which rack her emotionally and physically. When she seeks professional help, a team of psychiatrists study Carla’s situation seeking to unravel the source of her terror. Some sceptics in the team believe it to be female hysteria even insanity, but one kindly doctor, Phil Schneidermann (Ron Silver) is convinced of her sincerity and is determined to aid her plight with science and a parapsychological approach. At times genuinely terrifying, this film is somewhat dated, particularly in the special effects department, but interestingly was released just a year before Spielberg’s hugely successful “Poltergeist”, with which it shares many major themes. Hershey puts in a fine performance as an imperilled woman whose ordinary (if bleak) life is put under siege by forces beyond her comprehension. Although the film takes a somewhat dissatisfying turn mid-way through, it remains an interesting example of a science versus spirit movie and should prove a boon to horror buffs.

results.title: Sexy beast
results.creator_credit: Jonathan Glazer, Jeremy Thomas, Film Four International, Fox Searchlight Pictures and Kanzaman S A
results.brief_description: Gary (Ray Winstone) is a retired criminal, living in a luxurious Spanish villa with his ex-porn star wife and a couple of choice friends. The news of the impending arrival of Don (Ben Kingsley) - a former colleague of Don’s and a psychopath, puts the group on knife-edge. Don descends on the group like a hurricane - spewing foul language and oozing menace. His mission is to bring Gary back to London for a jewellery heist, masterminded by Teddy Bass (Ian MacShane). Gary’s refusal means little to Don as he cajoles, threatens, prods and manipulates Gary into taking the job. Don also works on intimidating the group, who are catatonic with fear and are on a helpless mission not to evoke Don’s anger. “Sexy beast” is a thriller/crime film that has a kinetic style that is at times reminiscent of Guy Ritchie. This film was Jonathon Glazer’s debut feature and Ben Kingsley was nominated for an Academy Award for Best Actor.

results.title: Wall Street
results.creator_credit: Oliver Stone, Edward Pressman and Twentieth Century-Fox Film Corporation
results.brief_description: Skinny neckties and double-breasted suits adorn Wall Street, which follows the path of Bud Fox (Charlie Sheen) into a milieu of corporate capitalism. He soon realises to stay in this new world with high-powered corporate espionage player Gordon Gekko (Michael Douglas) he must break a few rules. Gekko represents one of three father figures for Fox, the other two being his real father and his boss at the broking firm, all of whom give him constant advice. However, Fox’s overwhelming desire for gold cufflinks and brick-like cellular phones steer him down the path of corruption and greed and he soon realises the price of the pursuit of overnight success. Director Oliver Stone captures the hectic hum of the commerce world and encapsulates the greed that is synonymous with the rise of capitalism in the 1980s. Michael Douglas received an Oscar for Best Actor at the 1987 Academy Awards for his role as Gekko.

results.title: Voyage to the bottom on the sea
results.creator_credit: Irwin Allen, Twentieth Century-Fox Film Corporation and Windsor Productions
results.brief_description: Atmospheric adventure film and science fiction classic, “Voyage to the bottom of the sea” tells of a routine scientific exploration mission to the North Pole which goes horribly awry. The submarine crew, helmed by Admiral Harriman Nelson, a taciturn old-school navy man, are alarmed to discover that the Van Allen radiation belt has been ignited by a freak meteor shower, and the earth and sky are afire. Nelson, at loggerheads with his deputy, makes the inflammatory decision to extinguish the inferno by detonating a nuclear missile. Despite the censure of the United Nations, Nelson presses ahead with his scheme, whilst combating natural disasters, mutiny, giant sea creatures and a religious zealot on board. This film won many accolades for its special effects (state-of-the-art for its day), with a screenplay reflective of public concerns about potential apocalyptic nuclear disasters.

results.title: One night the moon [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Rachel Perkins, Aanya Whitehead, Kevin Lucas, Paul Humfress, ABC-TV (Australia), Australian Film Commission, New South Wales Film and Television Office, OzOpera and South Australian Film Corporation
results.brief_description: Directed by Rachel Perkins (Radiance), “One night the moon” is the story of a young girl living on an outback station with her parents, who goes missing one evening after becoming enchanted by the moon. After assembling a crew of police and volunteers, the missing child’s racist father will not allow Albert, a gifted Aboriginal tracker, on his land. Albert (Kelton Pell) laments the fate of the helpless child, whose traces are eliminated by the searchers, ruining her chance of survival. Perkins distinguishes this drama by having the majority of the film sung by the principal characters, using minimal dialogue and exposition throughout the narrative. Paul Kelly, who co-wrote the score with Kev Carmody and Mairead Hannan, tackles the difficult character of a bigot, blinded by a prejudice that threatens the life of his daughter. The parents’ marriage cracks under the strains of their child’s fate as the father turns to alcohol for comfort. Filmed in the Flinders Ranges, the cinematography in the film is quite stunning, giving a tremendous sense of the boundless landscape and incredible beauty of the outback. Based on events that occurred in 1932.

results.title: When Brendan met Trudy
results.creator_credit: Kieron Walsh, Lynda Myles, BBC Film Unit, Collins Avenue and Dendy Films
results.brief_description: Roddy Doyle (The Van, The Snapper, The Commitments) wrote the screenplay for this romantic comedy, set in modern day Dublin. Brendan is a shy schoolteacher, with few friends and no sex life to speak of who is obsessed with old films. He imagines himself at the centre of all his favourite roles, from “The Quiet man” to Godard’s “Breathless”. One evening whilst drinking in a local pub Brendan meets Trudy - an outgoing, vivacious and bubbly blonde - his complete opposite. Despite a disastrous first date Brendan and Trudy form an unlikely romance. Trudy’s secretive nature about her line of work leads Brendan to believe she is some kind of criminal, perhaps even a violent one. Brendan’s attempts to introduce Trudy to his snobbish family are disastrous and the pair struggle to keep their romance aflame. Characters enact scenes from Brendan’s favourite films, replete with subtitles. Part of the appeal of this film is the litany of film references and clips lifted from old movie classics. Directed by Kieron J. Walsh and starring Flora Montgomery and Peter McDonald.

results.title: The Shootist [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Don Siegel, M Frankovich, William Self and De Laurentiis Entertainment Group
results.brief_description: Set in 1901, John Wayne plays John Bernard Books - an ornery, aging gunfighter who returns to his old hometown, where his presence is unwanted and his way of life deemed obsolete. Informed that he has terminal cancer in advanced stages, Books determines to die quietly and with dignity, but several enemies decide to settle old scores before Books passes on. Boarding with a widow and his son, both of who form an attachment to him, Books tries to deter the hero-worshipping son from adopting his dangerous way of life. The film’s story serves as a parallel to Wayne’s own stellar career as a hero of the Western genre (and Wayne had cancer whilst shooting his final film). In this revisionist Western, Siegel frames the narrative within a series of identifiable dichotomies of the old/new West, horse power/machine engines, buggy/automobile and 19th/20th century mores. Also stars Jimmy Stewart as Books’ aging doctor and Lauren Bacall. Original music by Elmer Bernstein.

results.title: Flash Gordon [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Mike Hodges, Dino De Laurentiis, De Laurentiis Entertainment Group, Starling Films and Universal Pictures
results.brief_description: A fast paced, campy remake of the original 1936 Flash Gordon serial (315687) replete with a fierce rock score by Queen. An unseen force from outer space is causing all sorts of freak meteorological events. Flash (Sam Jones), quarterback of the New York Jets, and reporter Dale Arden (Melody Anderson) become reluctant passengers to outer-space when Zarkov (Topol), a discredited NASA scientist believes the Earth is being invaded by hostile aliens. Soon all are bound for the Planet Mongo, hurtling through a convenient Black Hole…there they have to find out who is causing all this extra-terrestrial trouble before the earth destructs. Captured by Ming the Merciless (Max Von Sydow) and hopelessly outnumbered by his slave minions, Flash must make allies of the flying Hawk Men led by Vultan (Brian Blessed) and the fierce Tree Men of Arboria ruled by the dashing Prince Barin (Timothy Dalton) before he can save Earth. Cast also includes Ornella Muti, Mariangelo Melato, Peter Wyngarde. Based on characters created by Alex Raymond in 1934.

results.title: Ulysses' gaze [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Theo Angelopoulos, Eric Heumann, Georgio Silvagni, Greek Film Centre, Mega Channel and Paradis Films
results.brief_description: Harvey Keitel is a Greek filmmaker who returns home after thirty-five years in America. He is in search of a lost film directed by the Manakias brothers, a film he believes to be the original “first film ever made”. His journey takes him on a journey through the Balkans, from Greece to Albania, from Bulgaria to Romania, a journey that ends in the besieged city of Sarajevo. On its release, “Ulysses Gaze” was criticised for its supposed ambivalent stance on the civil war in the former Yugoslavia. In hindsight, however, the film is an elegant and haunting eulogy on the death of utopia, and a film which acknowledges a much more complicated history to the Balkans than the one presented in the simplistic and moralistic films emerging from Western Europe. Ambitious and complex, “Ulysse’s Gaze” is also finally moving and heartbreaking; in its retelling of The Odyssey it passionately illuminates all that has been lost in the mad rush to replace culture with consumerism, to replace identity rooted in history with the fiction of perpetual recreation in cyberspace. There are scenes in this movie - the dismantling of Lenin’s statue, the final serenade in the Sarajevo fog - that confirm Angelopoulos as one of the leading film directors in the world today. Cast includes Erland Josephson, Maia Morgenstern and Thanassis Vengos.
